It sets `node-second-pass-next` for the start node.
Then `rotate-second-pass` iterates through the connected nodes again.
It sets the `at` facts for the new coordinates.
It checks `free` before setting `at`.
When the end node is reached in the second pass, `rotate-second-pass-end` is called.
It removes `rotating`.
